title: Challenge: Connecting RDS from EC2 Instance

tasks.title: Sign in to AWS Management Console

steps.text: Click Open Console to launch the AWS Management Console in a new browser tab.
On the login page: Retain the default Account ID (12-digit number; do not edit). Input the provided IAM Username and Password. Click Sign In.
Retain the default Account ID (12-digit number; do not edit).
Input the provided IAM Username and Password.
Click Sign In.
In the Console, select us-east-1 (N. Virginia) from the top-right region dropdown.
Sign in to AWS Management Console

XYZ Company needs its website server to talk to a secure database for storing customer data. Your job is to create a virtual computer (EC2), a MySQL database (RDS), connect them, set up a database with a table and data, test it, and clean up afterward. Here’s how to do each step super simply:
Create an EC2 Instance: Launch Ec2 I=insatnce with Image: Amazon linux 2023 kernel-6.1 AMI. Region: us-east-1. Instance type:t2 micro. With key-pair and port 22, port 80, port 3306 in security groups
Launch Ec2 I=insatnce with
Image: Amazon linux 2023 kernel-6.1 AMI.
Region: us-east-1.
Instance type:t2 micro.
With key-pair and port 22, port 80, port 3306 in security groups
Create an Amazon RDS Instance: Go to RDS > Create database, pick MySQL, choose Free Tier, same sg of EC2, Instance type: db.t3.micro Storage: 20 GiB Remember the username and password
Go to RDS > Create database, pick MySQL, choose Free Tier, same sg of EC2,
Instance type: db.t3.micro
Storage: 20 GiB
Remember the username and password
Create a Connection to the Amazon RDS Database from the EC2 Instance: Ssh your instance Install mysql . Connect mysql to the instance
Ssh your instance
Install mysql .
Connect mysql to the instance
Create a Database, Table, and Insert Data for Testing: Once connected to instance, create table and insert data to the table Fetch the data
Once connected to instance, create table and insert data to the table
Fetch the data
Make sure everything is correct. In the lab console, click Validate to check if the EC2 instance, RDS database, connection, and database setup are right.
Deleting AWS Resources.
Sign out of the AWS Management Console.
In the lab console, click End Challenge and wait for it to finish.
You’ve completed the RDS-EC2 connection challenge!
